title: ECDH, X25519, and Authenticated Key Agreement · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral
summary: A device protocol treats ECDH output as an encryption key, never authenticates peer keys, accepts malformed encodings, and reuses ephemeral keys indefinitely. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the forward secrecy ephemeral increment in authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Rotate and erase ephemeral private keys, prevent reuse, bind roles, and state when compromise does or does not expose prior sessions.
Authenticate peer identities and ephemeral public keys with signatures, certificates, PSKs, or an established trust channel.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 2391 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to authenticate and transcript-bind an ephemeral X25519 or approved ECDH exchange; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Build deterministic Go evidence to authenticate and transcript-bind an ephemeral X25519 or approved ECDH exchange; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Rotate and erase ephemeral private keys, prevent reuse, bind roles, and state when compromise does or does not expose prior sessions. A defensible solution applies Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript.

content.code:
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"crypto/ecdh"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	curve := ecdh.X25519()
	leftBytes, rightBytes := make([]byte, 32), make([]byte, 32)
	leftBytes[31], rightBytes[31] = 4, 5
	left, leftErr := curve.NewPrivateKey(leftBytes)
	right, rightErr := curve.NewPrivateKey(rightBytes)
	if leftErr != nil || rightErr != nil { panic("fixed fixture rejected") }
	leftSecret, errLeft := left.ECDH(right.PublicKey())
	rightSecret, errRight := right.ECDH(left.PublicKey())
	fmt.Printf("shared=%t errors=%t authenticated=false kdf_required=true\n", bytes.Equal(leftSecret, rightSecret), errLeft == nil && errRight == nil)
}
